title: S008 Phase 4: bootstrap wiring + local integration
description: Plan tasks 4.1-4.2. Epic photo_ops-861. Wire rabbitmq bus+result consumer+compose env, local end-to-end smoke.
close_reason: Phase 4 complete: rabbitmq bus + result consumer wired (TS topology mirrors Python), compose env, end-to-end smoke. LIVE RUN PASSED: upload->processing->ready with real variants + EXIF/GPS against the docker stack.

title: S008 Phase 3: delivery API (variants, detail, list fields)
description: Plan tasks 3.1-3.4. Epic photo_ops-861. Presigned GET, proto attrs+variant views+GetPhoto, photo-service GetPhoto+enriched list, api-gateway detail route+mapping.
close_reason: Phase 3 complete: presigned GET, proto attrs+variant-views+GetPhoto, photo-service GetPhoto+enriched ListPhotos (NOT_FOUND mapping), api-gateway detail route+mapping. All reviewed; gate + media-worker green.

title: S008 Phase 2: media-worker image processing pipeline
description: Plan tasks 2.1-2.5. Epic photo_ops-861. Config+storage, imaging (orient/fit/strip), EXIF/GPS+taken_at TZ, job handler with MinIO-metadata claim, rabbitmq adapter + consume loop.
close_reason: Phase 2 complete: media-worker config+storage, imaging (orient/fit/strip), EXIF/GPS+taken_at TZ, job handler (MinIO-metadata claim, failure-safe), rabbitmq adapter + consume loop. All reviewed; gate + media-worker green.

title: S008 Phase 1: photo-service data model, producer, finalize
description: Plan tasks 1.1-1.6. Epic photo_ops-861. Migration (variants/jobs/attributes), records, repo ops, publish job on complete, idempotent finalize, result consumer.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: 0002 migration (variants/jobs/attributes), record types, repository ops, publish-on-complete (charge-once guard), idempotent finalize, result consumer. All reviewed; full make gate + media-worker green.

title: S008 Phase 0: async contracts + messaging port + Python toolchain
description: Plan tasks 0.1-0.5. Part of epic photo_ops-861. Proto job/result messages (TS+Py codegen), broker-agnostic transport port + in-memory fake (TS+Py), media-worker Python toolchain+CI.
close_reason: Phase 0 complete: proto contracts (TS+Py codegen), broker-agnostic transport port + in-memory fake (TS+Py), media-worker python toolchain+CI. All tasks reviewed, gate-green.

title: Session 008: media-processing backend (async + EXIF/GPS + delivery API)
description: Implement the 2026-06-24 media-processing backend design: first async RabbitMQ workflow (broker-agnostic port, proto payloads), media-worker variant generation + EXIF/GPS extraction, photo-service producer/finalize with ProcessingJob idempotency, and owner-scoped delivery API (presigned GET, GetPhoto, enriched ListPhotos). Backend foundation for the rich gallery UI (session 009). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-24-photoops-media-processing-backend-design.md; Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-24-media-processing-backend.md. Subsumes/advances photo_ops-zg6 (structured logging/correlation id), photo_ops-78z (Python toolchain+CI), and overlaps photo_ops-4vg (integration tests). Defers reverse-geocoding (photo_ops-3iy).
acceptance_criteria: Upload completes -> job published -> media-worker generates thumbnail+preview + extracts EXIF/GPS -> result reported back -> photo-service writes variants/attributes -> status uploaded->processing->ready|failed; owner can fetch presigned preview; reprocessing/redelivery never duplicates variants or double-bills; make gate + make test-media-worker green.
close_reason: Session 008 media-processing backend COMPLETE. All 22 plan tasks done + reviewed; full gate + media-worker green; opus whole-branch review = ready to merge; live end-to-end smoke PASSED (make smoke-media). Follow-ups: photo_ops-di8 (adapter hardening).

title: T1: prune ephemeral permissions, consolidate broad reusable allow rules
description: settings.local.json holds ~60 one-shot entries (SHA-specific commit msgs, node -e probes, sed-with-ledger, echo exit:$? variants, git -C abspath dups). Replace with ~15 broad rules in shared settings.json; clear the local cruft.
close_reason: Implemented and verified (make gate green, branch 32bd6e1)

title: T1: add 'make gate' target + align CI to make targets + paths-ignore .beads
description: Single canonical gate = proto-check typecheck lint build test. CI steps call make targets (local==CI parity); add paths-ignore ['.beads/**'] so tracker-only pushes don't burn CI.
close_reason: Implemented and verified (make gate green, branch 32bd6e1)

title: Consolidate ergonomics analysis into docs/agent-ergonomics.md
description: Replace the four temporary session_00*_*.md root files with one committed report: data, permanent-vs-ephemeral split, repeated patterns, errors/abuses, tiered recommendations (T1/T2/T3) with rationale.
close_reason: Implemented and verified (make gate green, branch 32bd6e1)

title: Session 007: agent ergonomics tooling
description: Implement the T1/T2/T3 ergonomics improvements distilled from session 005/006 bash-command analysis: make gate + CI parity + beads paths-ignore (T1); permission allowlist cleanup (T1); repo-root-safe SDD wrapper (T2); beads-jsonl churn mitigation (T2); AGENTS.md working conventions (T3); consolidated docs/agent-ergonomics.md report.
close_reason: Session 008 delivered: docs/agent-ergonomics.md + T1 (make gate, CI parity, beads paths-ignore, permission cleanup) + T2 (scripts/sdd, .gitattributes) + T3 (AGENTS.md conventions). Follow-up qsl tracks upstream bd export ordering.

title: Session 006: Quality Gate
description: Put a green quality gate (local + CI) over the existing TypeScript slice before session 007 introduces the first async workflow and the Python media-worker. Scope: typecheck (yl7), CI pipeline (7jh), proto drift check folded into CI (9h5). Deferred: real ESLint (p8y), mise toolchain pinning. No product features.
close_reason: Quality gate complete incl. ESLint: typecheck (yl7), proto-check (9h5), CI (7jh), lint (p8y). All gate steps green in CI. Follow-up 1yb.1 detached.

title: Session 00a: improve AI-agent working conditions
description: Session type 00a (agent working conditions, third numbering scheme alongside NNN feature sessions and 00i reviews). Goal: not to close the quality/observability backlog, but to make it cheap to close in future sessions without this session's context. Deliverables: knowledge-placement policy; hierarchical CLAUDE.md (two-section: Local context / Local invariants) for real services + key dirs, stubs for scaffolds; slim root CLAUDE.md pointing to canonical AGENTS.md (dedup); freshness discipline rule in AGENTS.md (no automation); seed bd remember with durable decisions (mise toolchain, dev-env strategy, session-numbering, now/deferred prioritization); ADR-0002 toolchain pinning with mise; sessions/README.md documenting the three session-numbering schemes; a research report evaluating agent-coding support tooling (graphlens and alternatives) against this stack with follow-up issues (research only, no adoption); design doc + session brief + interim report. Optional stretch: adopt selected tooling if context remains.
close_reason: Session 005 delivered: knowledge model, hierarchical CLAUDE.md, ADR-0002, bd memories, flat numbering, settings hygiene, agent-tooling + Claude Code practices research. Merged to main.

title: Add typecheck gate (tsc --noEmit) across TS services
description: Problem: no service has a typecheck script; type errors only surface at full build. Why now: cheap, immediate signal on the existing TS slice. What: add a 'typecheck' script (tsc --noEmit) to api-gateway, identity-service, photo-service, web (and scaffolds where cheap), a root aggregate 'pnpm -r typecheck', and a Makefile target. Consumed by the CI pipeline issue.
close_reason: typecheck scripts + make target added and verified green

title: Add CI pipeline (typecheck, tests, build, contract smoke)
description: Problem: no .github/workflows exists. Vitest specs and smoke scripts run only manually, so nothing enforces a green state before session branches merge into main. Why now: pays off on the existing working TS slice regardless of future service shape; we merge branches into main and need a regression gate. What: GitHub Actions workflow on push/PR that runs pnpm install, proto generation, typecheck, build, vitest across apps/*, and scripts/test-smoke-upload-contract.sh. Start minimal (TS services only); Go/Python services wired later under a separate deferred issue.
close_reason: CI workflow added; full Quality gate job observed green on session-006-quality-gate (run 28012990377): proto-check, typecheck, build, test all pass

title: Tighten agent rules and Makefile commands

close_reason: Trimmed AGENTS.md to durable project rules and added canonical Makefile workflow targets.

acceptance_criteria: docker compose starts postgres/minio/minio-init/rabbitmq; database isolation query returns cluster_db, connector_db, photo_db, publication_db, usage_db.

acceptance_criteria: Proto files exist for photo, cluster, publication, usage, connector, and common; pnpm install and pnpm proto generate TypeScript files under packages/proto-ts/src.
close_reason: Task 2 proto contracts and TypeScript generation verified and reviewed

title: Session 002: root tooling and docs frame
description: Create the root monorepo frame for the first executable PhotoOps architecture slice: package.json, pnpm-workspace.yaml, .env.example, Makefile, README, architecture docs, roadmap, and ADR. This corresponds to Task 1 in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-21-architecture-frame-upload-slice.md.
acceptance_criteria: Root workspace files exist; README documents upload/list frame; Makefile contains install/proto/build/test/lint/dev/down/logs/status/migrate-photo/smoke-upload commands; verification command from plan exits 0.
close_reason: Task 1 root tooling and documentation frame verified and reviewed

title: Make live-stack validation first-class: one-shot make smoke-stack
description: s008's biggest lesson: a green 'make gate' AND a clean whole-branch review missed THREE real bugs (worker RabbitMQ boot-race crash, the _lat/_lon proto3-optional leak, the smoke's own assertion) that ONLY the live 'make smoke-media' caught -- mocks share the code's assumptions, the real transport does not. Make the highest-value validation cheap and reliable: a 'make smoke-stack' target that (a) df headroom check, (b) builds only the services the smoke needs, (c) up -d + wait for health, (d) make migrate, (e) make smoke-media, (f) teardown -- with full build output to a LOG FILE, not '| tail' (which ate the failure in s008). Also dodges the detached-build-log-loss and ENOSPC frictions (#3/#4). See docs/agent-ergonomics.md (s008 friction #8).

title: Polyglot quality gate: fold media-worker into make gate (or add make gate-all)
description: Session 008 (first polyglot session) finding, see docs/agent-ergonomics.md. 'make gate' = proto-check typecheck lint build test is TS-only; the Python media-worker is verified ad-hoc (make test-media-worker ran 8x OUTSIDE the gate; implementers ran lint/test manually). No single command verifies the whole repo now that it is polyglot. Fix: extend 'make gate' to also run lint-media-worker + test-media-worker, or add 'make gate-all' (TS gate + Python) and point CI at it. Highest-payoff ergonomics finding of s008.
close_reason: Folded the Python media-worker into 'make gate' via a new 'gate-media' target (lint-media-worker + test-media-worker). 'make gate' now verifies the whole polyglot repo in one command; full gate passes end-to-end (TS + 33 pytest, ruff/mypy clean). Synced AGENTS.md and docs/agent-ergonomics.md.

title: T3: add agent working conventions to AGENTS.md
description: Doc-only: no post-commit confirmation tails; Co-Authored-By trailer required (hook slot owned by beads); bd create --json for ID capture; rely on Bash output truncation; verify tree assumptions by command over whole repo; make gate is the canonical pre-push check.
close_reason: Implemented and verified (make gate green, branch 32bd6e1)

title: T2: mitigate .beads/issues.jsonl churn (.gitattributes + bd follow-up)
description: Export reorders _type:memory lines with no semantic change -> permanent dirty status. bd export has no sort flag. Add .gitattributes (merge=union, linguist-generated); document convention; file follow-up to request stable export ordering upstream.
close_reason: Implemented and verified (make gate green, branch 32bd6e1)

title: T2: add repo-root-safe scripts/sdd wrapper (brief/package/base/done)
description: Self-contained SDD helper anchored at git rev-parse --show-toplevel (fixes cwd bug that broke review-package twice in s006); records BASE, generates brief + review package, updates ledger. Removes ~3 cmds/task and the BASE footgun.
close_reason: Implemented and verified (make gate green, branch 32bd6e1)

title: Add structured logging baseline across api-gateway/identity/photo
description: Problem: no centralized or structured logs; only 'make logs' on raw stdout. This is the biggest gap for support/diagnosis. Why now: foundation of observability that helps the existing services immediately and is independent of future service shape. What: structured JSON logging with a request/correlation id propagated web -> api-gateway -> identity/photo (incl. across gRPC), consistent levels, and no secrets in logs. Metrics/tracing are a separate deferred issue.

title: Add hermetic integration tests for identity/photo (ephemeral Postgres/MinIO)
description: Problem: the only end-to-end coverage is manual bash smoke scripts that need a live 'make dev' stack; changes cannot be verified hermetically. Why now: the identity and photo services have real DB/MinIO behavior worth pinning. What: integration tests using testcontainers (or compose-in-CI) that spin up ephemeral Postgres and MinIO, apply migrations, and exercise signup/login and upload-intent/complete/list. Scope to existing services only; future services get their own coverage when they take shape.

title: Add proto generation drift check
description: Problem: generated ts-proto code is checked in but nothing verifies it matches the .proto sources, so stale generation can land silently. Why: protects the proto-first contract boundary. What: a check (Makefile target + CI step) that runs 'make proto' and fails if 'git diff --exit-code' shows the generated package changed.
close_reason: make proto-check added; folded into CI in Task 3

title: Set up real ESLint linting across services
description: No ESLint configuration exists in the repo; lint is currently a no-op everywhere. Decide on and wire a shared ESLint (flat config) setup for the NestJS services and web, then make lint scripts actually run it. Deferred from Session I fortification review as larger than a cheap fix.
close_reason: ESLint flat-config gate landed; promise-safety rules error; CI Lint step green (run 28024636554)

title: Fix broken make lint with no-op until ESLint is configured
description: make lint exits non-zero: api-gateway, identity-service, photo-service declare 'eslint src --ext .ts' but eslint is not installed and no ESLint config exists anywhere; web uses deprecated interactive 'next lint'. Found during Session I fortification review verification. Cheap fix: set the four real services' lint scripts to the same no-op convention used by scaffolds so the canonical make lint target passes honestly.
close_reason: Set the four real service lint scripts to the project no-op convention; make lint now exits 0. Real ESLint setup deferred to photo_ops-p8y.

title: Add service readiness checks
description: Application services should expose readiness checks that verify their owned dependencies, such as database connectivity for data-owning services and MinIO access for photo-service. This was identified during Session I fortification review and deferred to avoid expanding scope.

title: Prepare identity branch for review
description: Update README for identity branch state, add manual e2e scenarios in chat/docs, add project rule requiring e2e scenario approval before implementation, and write session 003 summary.
close_reason: Updated README, e2e scenarios, project workflow rule, and session 003 report before review.

title: Add identity users and upload ownership
description: Implement identity-service, e-mail/password signup/login, HTTP-only session cookie handling in api-gateway, user_id ownership in photo-service, web auth UI, and two-user smoke verification.
close_reason: Implemented identity-service, auth sessions, and user-scoped upload/list ownership.

title: Design domain model and users
description: Plan the domain model documentation and user ownership/authentication changes before implementation. Scope: current/projected domain model outside feature branch, then user-owned entities and e-mail/password login in a feature branch. No code changes until the design is approved.
close_reason: Domain model and identity/ownership work shipped in Session 003; issue was left in_progress. Closing stale state during Session I fortification review.

title: Session 002: remaining service scaffolds
description: Create health-only scaffolds for media-worker, cluster-service, publication-service, usage-service, and connector-service with explicit 501 responses for non-health paths. This corresponds to Task 8 in the upload slice plan.
acceptance_criteria: All scaffold services have package/module files; health endpoints return status ok; unimplemented paths return 501/not_implemented; TypeScript scaffolds build and Go scaffold tests.
close_reason: Task 8 remaining service scaffolds verified and reviewed

title: media-worker make targets: reuse .venv instead of recreating each run
description: test-media-worker and lint-media-worker run 'python3 -m venv .venv && pip install -q -e .[dev]' unconditionally on every invocation -> repeated install on each lint/test loop. Guard it: [ -d .venv ] || create; reinstall only when pyproject.toml changed. Minor perf, recurs every Python loop. See docs/agent-ergonomics.md (s008 friction #7).

title: Add docker compose convenience make targets + DC var for diagnostics
description: s008 retyped the long prefix 'docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.yml --env-file .env ...' 17x for diagnostics not covered by make dev/down/reset/logs/status (which are all-services, happy-path). Add a DC make-var and targets for the gaps: ps -a, logs svc=<x>, sh/exec svc=<x>, restart svc=<x>, targeted 'up -d <subset>'. See docs/agent-ergonomics.md (s008 friction #5).

title: RabbitMqBus production hardening (reconnect, publish flow-control, shared topology constants)
description: Follow-up from session 008 Task 4.1 review. The TS+Python RabbitMQ adapters are skeleton-grade: (1) no automatic reconnect — a dropped broker connection stops consumption until process restart (currently only logged); decide crash-restart vs reconnect-with-backoff. (2) channel.publish/basic_publish return value (flow-control 'false' / drain) is ignored — at high publish rates messages could be dropped silently; await drain. (3) logical topology names ('photo.process','photo.result') are inlined string literals on both sides rather than a shared topology.ts constant (DX/rename risk). Epic photo_ops-861.

title: Bump GitHub Actions versions off deprecated Node 20
description: CI run 28041644864 warned: actions/checkout@v4, actions/setup-node@v4, pnpm/action-setup@v4 target Node.js 20 (deprecated; GitHub forces them onto Node 24). Bump each to the latest major that targets Node 24 (e.g. actions/checkout@v5, actions/setup-node@v5, pnpm/action-setup@v5 or current) in .github/workflows/ci.yml, then confirm a green run with no Node-20 annotation. Benign today; do before the warning becomes a hard failure.

title: Harden proto drift check against untracked generated files
description: Follow-up from session-006 final review. make proto-check uses 'git diff --exit-code -- packages/proto-ts', which ignores UNTRACKED files. The realistic regression (editing a .proto without regenerating tracked output) IS caught. But adding a brand-new proto package whose generated .ts files are not yet git-added would pass the check green despite uncommitted generated output. Fix options: use 'git status --porcelain -- packages/proto-ts' (catches untracked), or 'git add -N packages/proto-ts' before the diff. Low priority; the in-scope drift case is already covered.

title: Add PostToolUse hook to auto-run quality gates after edits
description: From docs/claude-code-practices.md (session 005, deferred). A PostToolUse hook on Write|Edit could auto-run a fast quality gate (typecheck/lint/targeted vitest) so regressions surface without a full CI pipeline. BLOCKED: make lint is currently a no-op (photo_ops-p8y) and there is no typecheck script yet (photo_ops-yl7) — auto-running a no-op is pointless. Wire this only after a real gate exists; keep it fast and non-noisy.

title: Evaluate adopting Serena for code navigation
description: From docs/agent-tooling-research.md: LSP-backed MCP server, symbol-path editing for TS/Go/Python (23K stars, v1.5.3 May 2026); no proto support out of box but Buf LSP can fill the gap. Integration cost: low (install via uv, one config line). Pilot on the existing TS services first; decide before wiring into agent workflow.
close_reason: Not chosen: piloting codebase-memory-mcp (photo_ops-cdk) instead — single static binary, 158 languages incl. proto, auto-detects Claude Code, very-low integration cost vs Serena needing uv + no out-of-box proto support. Revisit only if the cdk pilot fails to deliver.

title: Evaluate adopting codebase-memory-mcp for code navigation
description: From docs/agent-tooling-research.md: single static binary, 158 languages (TS/Go/Python/proto confirmed), 14 MCP tools including cross-service HTTP linking; auto-detects Claude Code. Integration cost: very low (download binary, run install). Pilot on the existing TS services first; decide before wiring into agent workflow.
notes: Chosen over Serena (photo_ops-02q, closed 2026-06-24) for the code-nav pilot: lower integration cost (single binary, no uv), confirmed proto support, auto-detects Claude Code. Scope: pilot on existing TS services first; decide before wiring into the agent workflow.

title: Backfill ADR-004 (connectors as distribution) and ADR-005 (append-only billing ledger)
description: Problem: project_description.md references ADR-004 'connectors as distribution channels' and ADR-005 'append-only billing ledger', but docs/adr/ only contains 0001-architecture-frame.md. Why: these are load-bearing decisions for the connector and usage stages and should be recorded before that work starts. What: write ADR-004 and ADR-005 in docs/adr/ matching the existing ADR format.

title: Add toolchain and CI wiring for Go and Python services
description: Problem: cluster-service (Go) and media-worker (Python) are health-only scaffolds with no real lint/test/build gates; CI starts TS-only. Why deferred (per project state): these services have no real behavior yet and their technical form is not final, so investing in their toolchain now is premature. What (when unblocked): go vet/test and ruff+pytest (or equivalent), wired into the CI pipeline. Blocked-until: the respective service gains real, testable behavior in an approved session.

title: Add metrics and tracing (OpenTelemetry) once services take shape
description: Problem: no metrics or distributed tracing exist. Why deferred: full observability value depends on the not-yet-final service topology (Go cluster-service, Python media-worker, async RabbitMQ flows). What (later): OpenTelemetry traces and basic RED metrics across services and gRPC/queue boundaries. Blocked-until: at least one of cluster-service/media-worker has real behavior and async contracts start to exist. Builds on the structured-logging baseline.

title: Document future production infrastructure shape
description: Capture production gaps for deployment, TLS, secrets, storage policies, backups, observability, and migration execution after the MVP path stabilizes.

title: Request stable bd export ordering for .beads/issues.jsonl
description: bd export reshuffles _type:memory lines with no semantic change on every command, producing permanent dirty git status. bd export has no --sort/--order flag (verified s008). Mitigated locally via .gitattributes (merge=union, linguist-generated) but the root fix is deterministic export order. Action: open/track an upstream beads request for stable id-sorted export, or confirm a config knob exists in a newer bd. Until then the convention in AGENTS.md (do not hand-firefight the reorder) applies.
notes: Decision (2026-06-24): keep as thin upstream tracker (not closed). Local pain already mitigated via .gitattributes (merge=union, linguist-generated). Action when picked up: verify a newer bd has a --sort/--order export flag; if not, file an upstream beads request for deterministic id-sorted export.

title: Evaluate custom Claude Code subagents (.claude/agents) for polyglot side-tasks
description: From docs/claude-code-practices.md (session 005, deferred). Candidates: proto-auditor (Read/Glob — naming/breaking-change checks via buf), migration-auditor (Read/Glob — unsafe SQL ops), go-vet (Bash go vet in cluster-service, isolates Go toolchain from main TS context). Generic Explore/general-purpose already cover most needs, so adopt only if a custom def clearly earns its upkeep. CONSTRAINT: do NOT use isolation: worktree — this project bans git worktrees (beads conflict).
notes: Decision (2026-06-24): keep deferred (not won't-do). Generic Explore/general-purpose agents cover current needs. Revisit when Go (cluster-service) / Python (media-worker) gain real behavior AND a repeating polyglot side-task (proto-audit, migration-audit, go vet) clearly earns a custom def. CONSTRAINT: no isolation:worktree (project bans worktrees).

title: Decide reverse-geocoding approach during clustering stage
description: Backlog reminder, not a blocker. Clustering works without geocoding; reverse geocoding is informative (place tags) and may need an external API key or a manual/offline fallback. Decision is consciously deferred until the clustering stage. What: when clustering is implemented, choose between an external geocoding API (provision a key) vs a simplified/offline fallback, honoring the requirement to keep coordinates and continue if geocoding is unavailable.

title: Choose migration runner workflow
description: Local migrations are currently manual per service. Decide whether to keep Makefile-driven psql migrations or introduce a service-owned migration runner before schema count grows.
notes: Decision (2026-06-24): consciously deferred. Keep manual Makefile/psql per-service migrations for now. Trigger to revisit: when migration/schema count grows enough that manual ordering becomes error-prone — then choose Makefile-driven vs service-owned runner (node-pg-migrate / golang-migrate).

value: PhotoOps proto/gRPC + messaging gotchas (session 008): (1) packages/proto-ts is SOURCE-ONLY (main: src/index.ts, no build; imports uninstalled @bufbuild/protobuf) — NOT runtime-consumable. For runtime proto encode/decode use protobufjs loadSync over the .proto (camelCase fields, toObject longs:String->bigint, enums:Number) as in apps/photo-service/src/photo/processing.codec.ts; the Python worker uses generated _pb2 (snake_case). (2) proto3 'optional' compiles to a synthetic '_field' oneof; @grpc/proto-loader surfaces it as '_lat'/'_lon' props that leak through a spread — strip underscore-prefixed keys in the gateway mapPhoto. (3) Both RabbitMQ adapters (TS amqplib + Python pika) connect at startup with bounded retry to survive the broker boot race; canonical topology (exchange/queue/DLX/DLQ, dlq routing-key=N) is mirrored EXACTLY on both sides — change both together or assertQueue fails PRECONDITION_FAILED.

Decision (ADR-0002), not yet implemented: pin the toolchain with mise (.tool-versions) for node/pnpm/go/python; buf stays a pnpm devDependency. JS toolchain is hermetic via pnpm; runtime/integration infra runs in Docker. Planned single canonical 'make bootstrap'. See docs/adr/0002-toolchain-pinning-with-mise.md.

Knowledge placement: AGENTS.md = canonical agent rules; root CLAUDE.md = thin Claude-Code pointer; nested CLAUDE.md (## Local context / ## Local invariants) = local code knowledge for real services + key dirs, stubs for scaffolds; bd remember = durable file-independent facts; docs/adr + docs/superpowers = decisions and per-session design. Nested CLAUDE.md freshness is discipline (update in the same commit), not automated.

Prioritization principle: invest in tooling that pays off on the existing TypeScript slice now (CI, typecheck, eslint, structured logs); defer anything that depends on not-yet-built Go cluster-service or Python media-worker until those services gain real behavior.
PhotoOps project rule: do not use git worktrees; use regular git feature branches with git switch -c because worktrees conflict with beads.
